name: rustworkx (IBM Rust-Backed Python Graph Library)

performance.notes: Rust backend via PyO3; comparable to igraph; faster than NetworkX; memory-efficient

agent_readiness.notes: IBM's Rust-backed Python graph library (formerly retworkx). import rustworkx as rx. g = rx.PyGraph(). g.add_node({'name': 'A'}). g.add_edge(0, 1, {'weight': 1.0}). rx.dijkstra_shortest_paths(g, 0). rx.betweenness_centrality(g). rx.connected_components(g). rx.is_planar(g). DAG support: PyDiGraph. Topological sort. Supports arbitrary Python objects as node/edge data. Used in Qiskit for quantum circuit DAGs. Apache-2.0 license (permissive). NetworkX-like but faster.

gotchas: Formerly called 'retworkx' — both names appear in docs
Smaller community/ecosystem than NetworkX or igraph
Not a drop-in NetworkX replacement (different API)
Less graph algorithms than NetworkX (but growing)
best_when: High-performance graph processing with Apache-2.0 license (vs igraph GPL)
DAG processing (topological sort, critical path) in Qiskit/quantum circuits
When NetworkX is too slow and permissive license needed
Graphs with complex Python object data on nodes/edges
avoid_when: Comprehensive algorithm coverage needed (igraph or NetworkX better)
summary: rustworkx is IBM's Rust-backed Python graph library (via PyO3). rx.PyGraph() and rx.PyDiGraph() for undirected/directed graphs. Arbitrary Python objects as node/edge data. Algorithms: Dijkstra, betweenness, connected components, topological sort, planarity testing. Apache-2.0 (permissive vs igraph's GPL). Used in Qiskit for quantum circuit DAGs. Faster than NetworkX; comparable to igraph. Formerly called retworkx.
